entries.title: Tailwind CSS - Rapidly build modern websites without ever leaving your ...
entries.summary:

* Dark mode is enabled by prefixing colors with `dark:`.
* CSS variables are used for customizing themes, with examples like `--font-sans` and `--color-mint-100`.
* Classes can be composed to build complex designs, such as `flex flex-col items-center rounded-2xl`.
* Utility classes can be used to apply filters, such as `blur-sm` and `brightness-150`.
* Tailwind CSS supports modern CSS features for an enjoyable developer experience.
* Customization is done through CSS variables, allowing for easy theme changes.
* The framework is supported by partners and sponsors, enabling full-time maintenance by a team of designers and engineers.
* Tailwind CSS uses OKLCH color model for more vibrant wide gamut colors.
* Pre-defined color palette includes 13 colors with 10 shades each.
* CSS grid layout is supported through grid utilities.
* Transitions and animations can be customized using utility classes.
* Cascade layers are used to avoid specificity issues.
* Logical properties support multiple language text directions (LTR and RTL).
* Container queries allow children to adapt to changes in container size.
* Gradients can be created using utility classes.

entries.title: Introduction to Tailwind CSS - GeeksforGeeks
entries.summary:
* Tailwind CSS is a utility-first framework for styling websites directly in HTML.
* Uses small, reusable utility classes for styling, reducing custom CSS needs.
* Applies styles directly in HTML using utility classes, reducing context switching.
* Predefined utility classes eliminate the need for custom CSS class naming.
* Rapid UI development using ready-made utility classes.
* Consistent and reusable design system that scales easily.
* Supports responsive design with mobile-first approach and utility prefixes.
* Built-in dark mode support using variants.
* Highly customizable through configuration files.
* Integrates seamlessly with React, Vue, and Next.js.
* Advantages of Tailwind CSS include minimal CSS output, easy customization, and built-in responsiveness.
* Tailwind CSS eliminates the need for complex class names and custom CSS naming conventions.
* Utility classes enable flexible design changes and reusable components without extra CSS.
* Scoped styling applies styles locally to elements, avoiding global CSS side effects.
* Limitations of Tailwind CSS include long class lists, a learning curve, and the need for build setup.
* Tailwind CSS can make HTML markup less semantic due to styling-heavy classes.
* Production usage of Tailwind CSS requires configuration and build tools.
* Tailwind CSS enables creation of mobile-friendly layouts using responsive utility classes.
* It reduces large custom stylesheets, keeping the codebase lightweight and maintainable.

entries.title: How to Install Tailwind CSS: Complete Setup Guide
entries.summary:
* Node.js version 14 or higher is required to install Tailwind CSS.
* npm or yarn is needed for package management.
* Basic familiarity with command line and HTML/CSS is assumed.
* Tailwind CSS v4 uses a utility-first approach with small, single-purpose class names.
* The framework is built on Lightning CSS, a Rust-based compiler, for faster builds.
* Setup is now a single line in the CSS file.
* For framework projects (React, Vue, Next.js), a project should be scaffolded before adding Tailwind.
* The standalone CLI is suitable for plain HTML projects or setups without a bundler like Vite.
* PostCSS configuration and additional plugins are not required for the standalone CLI.
* Tailwind CSS v4 takes advantage of modern CSS features like cascade layers and registered custom properties.
* To install Tailwind CSS, run `npm install tailwindcss @tailwindcss/cli`.
* Create a `src/input.css` file and add `@import "tailwindcss";` to use Tailwind.
* Run the development build with `npx @tailwindcss/cli -i ./src/input.css -o ./dist/output.css --watch`.
* For production build, use `npx @tailwindcss/cli -i ./src/input.css -o ./dist/output.css --minify`.
* To install Tailwind with Vite, run `npm install tailwindcss @tailwindcss/vite`.
* Configure Vite plugin by adding `import tailwindcss from '@tailwindcss/vite';` to `vite.config.js`.
* Add `tailwindcss()` to the `plugins` array in `vite.config.js`.
* Import Tailwind in main CSS file with `@import "tailwindcss";`.
* Tailwind v4 automatical

* JSX is an extension to JavaScript syntax, similar to HTML, used for structuring component rendering.
* JSX is converted to JavaScript code during compilation.
* React can be used for server-side rendering (SSR) to improve application performance.
* SSR allows rendering React components on the server and sending the resulting HTML to the client.
* React supports universal loading, rendering identical HTML on both server and client.
* React can be used to develop native apps for Android and iOS using React Native.
* `useState` is a hook used to declare state variables in functional components.
* React components can be written in pure JavaScript, but JSX is commonly used.
* React is designed for building user interfaces, not a complete application library.
* Unidirectional data flow is a core concept in React, contrasting with bidirectional flow in AngularJS.
* Flux architecture is used to support unidirectional data flow, featuring actions, dispatchers, and stores.
* Flux can be considered a variant of the observer pattern.
* React components should not directly modify props, but use callback functions to create actions.
* Redux and MobX are popular alternatives to Flux for state management.
* useReducer is a React hook introduced in 16.8, enabling local state management similar to Redux.

entries.title: WebSocket - Wikipedia
entries.summary:
* WebSocket protocol enables full-duplex communication over TCP port 80.
* WebSocket aims to solve problems of Comet implementation inefficiency for small messages.
* The WebSocket protocol was first versioned in June 2008 by Michael Carter.
* WebSocket interface allows bidirectional communication with a WebSocket server.
* WebSocket URL scheme must be ws, wss, http, or https.
* WebSocket constructor: `ws = new WebSocket(url [, protocols])`.
* `ws.send(data)` method sends data message, where data can be string, Blob, ArrayBuffer, or ArrayBufferView.
* WebSocket events: onopen, onmessage, onclose, onerror.
* RFC 6455 finalized the WebSocket protocol in December 2011.
* RFC 7692 introduced compression extension to WebSocket using the DEFLATE algorithm.
* WebSocket clients must mask all frames sent to the server.
* Control frames are unfragmentable and have an opcode >= 8.
* The FIN bit indicates whether a frame is the last one in a message.
* The OPCODE field determines the type of WebSocket frame.
* Payload size is determined by the first byte of the frame.
* If payload size is 126, the actual size is a 2-byte unsigned integer.
* If payload size is 127, the actual size is an 8-byte unsigned integer.
* The minimum number of bits must be used to represent the payload size.
* Payload size is calculated using bitwise operations (e.g. `byte1 & 0b111_1111`).
* The `struct.unpack` function is used to unpack binary data from the connection.

entries.title: WebSocket API: Events, Methods & Properties
entries.summary:
* The WebSocket API uses an asynchronous, event-driven model.
* `close()` method initiates the closing handshake or closes the connection.
* `close()` parameters: `code` (optional, default: 1000) and `reason` (optional, max 123 bytes UTF-8).
* Four event types: `open`, `message`, `error`, `close`.
* Events can be handled using `onevent` properties (e.g. `onopen`) or `addEventListener()`.
* `onopen` event: fired when the WebSocket connection is established.
* `onmessage` event: fired when data is received from the server, `event.data` contains the message payload.
* `onerror` event: fired when an error occurs, but doesn't provide detailed error information for security reasons.
* `binaryType` property determines the type of binary data received: `'arraybuffer'` or `'blob'`.

entries.title: FastAPI WebSockets Tutorial: Real Time Python
entries.summary:
* FastAPI has built-in support for WebSockets, enabling real-time communication.
* WebSockets create a persistent, two-way channel between client and server.
* To use WebSockets with FastAPI, install `fastapi`, `uvicorn`, and `websockets` using pip.
* Define a WebSocket route using the `@app.websocket` decorator.
* Handle WebSocket connections using `await websocket.accept()`, `await websocket.receive_text()`, and `await websocket.send_text()`.
* Use Uvicorn to run the FastAPI application with `uvicorn main:app --reload`.
* Test WebSocket connections using a simple HTML page with JavaScript.
* Manage multiple clients by storing active connections in a list and handling `WebSocketDisconnect` events.
* Use `ws://localhost:8000/ws` as the WebSocket endpoint URL.
* FastAPI provides automatic documentation and data validation using standard Python type hints.
* Store active WebSocket connections in a list to manage clients.
* Use `WebSocketDisconnect` exception to handle client disconnections.
* Create a `ConnectionManager` class to organize connection logic.
* Use `async`/`await` for asynchronous WebSocket operations.
* Validate incoming JSON data with Pydantic models (e.g., `BaseModel`).
* Use `websocket.receive_text()` to receive text messages from clients.
* Use `websocket.send_text()` to send text messages to clients.
* Use `websocket.receive_json()` to receive JSON data from clients.
* Broadcast messages to all connected clients using a loop.

entries.title: Advanced Tailwind CSS Patterns That Will Change How You Build UIs
entries.summary:
* Use CSS variables for design tokens, e.g., `:root { --color-brand: 16 185 129; }`.
* Define theme colors using `rgb(var(--color-brand) / <alpha-value>)`.
* Utilize the `cva` pattern from `class-variance-authority` for component variation.
* Define component variants using `cva` options, e.g., `variants: { intent: { primary: 'bg-brand-500 text-white hover:bg-brand-600' } }`.
* Set default variants for components, e.g., `defaultVariants: { intent: 'primary', size: 'md' }`.
* Extend Tailwind theme using `module.exports = { theme: { extend: { colors: { brand: '...' } } } }`.
* Use `cva` to generate class names for components, e.g., `const button = cva('inline-flex items-center rounded-lg font-medium', { ... })`.
* Leverage Tailwind's built-in utility classes for styling, e.g., `bg-brand-500`, `text-white`, `hover:bg-brand-600`.
* Define custom color palettes using CSS variables, e.g., `.dark { --color-brand: 52 211 153; }`.
* Use `class-variance-authority` to manage complex component variations and reduce CSS bloat.

entries.title: React Server Components - patterns.dev
entries.summary:
* React Server Components (RSC) can reduce bundle size by 20-40% by keeping non-interactive code on the server.
* Imports inside Server Components contribute zero to the client-side bundle.
* Data-fetching code, auth checks, and query builders stay on the server with RSC.
* Client components should be small and only handle interactivity.
* File conventions in Next.js App Router include `page.tsx`, `layout.tsx`, `loading.tsx`, `error.tsx`, and `route.ts`.
* Server Components are suitable for non-interactive page elements like navigation, footer, and headings.
* Lists of content from a database or CMS can use Server Components with inline data fetching.
* Forms should use a Client Component for the form fields and submit state, calling a Server Action.
* Components using `useState`, `useEffect`, or event handlers should be Client Components.
* Heavy libraries used only for rendering output should be used in Server Components to avoid adding to the client-side bundle.
* Use `useState`, `useEffect`, or event handlers in Client Components.
* Keep heavy libraries for rendering output in Server Components.
* Use `next/dynamic` for interactive widgets to reduce initial load cost.
* Treat `'use client'` as the exception, declared at the leaves of the tree.
* Use `use cache` directive to mark functions or components as cacheable.
* In Next.js 15, APIs like `cookies()`, `headers()`, and `searchParams` are async.
* RSC support is being adopted by frameworks like Waku, RedwoodJS, and Remi

entries.title: React Server Components: A comprehensive guide
entries.summary:
* React Server Components (RSCs) allow for server rendering at the component level.
* RSCs introduce a new mental model, enabling components to span both server and client realms.
* Client-side rendering (CSR) can lead to slow websites and poor SEO performance.
* Server-side rendering (SSR) and static site generation (SSG) can improve rendering performance.
* SSR renders pages at request time, while SSG occurs at build time.
* Hydration is the process of making a server-rendered UI interactive with client-side JavaScript.
* RSCs can seamlessly interweave with Client Components for a blend of server-side efficiency and client-side interactivity.
* React 18 supports concurrent rendering and RSCs.
* Next 14 and 15 RC enhance RSC integration.
* RSCs can improve Core Web Vitals and SEO performance.
* React Server Components allow UI rendering and caching on the server.
* Component logic, such as data fetching and database mutations, is executed exclusively on the server.
* Server Components can import Client Components for dynamic client-side interactivity.
* Server Components can asynchronously fetch data and pre-render content entirely on the server.
* The generated HTML template is streamed into the client-side React tree.
* Server Components never re-render, resulting in faster page loading times.
* Using Server Components reduces the total JavaScript bundle size and improves page load time.
* Advantages of Server Components include improved performance, performant SEO, enhanc

entries.title: Architecture of Docker - GeeksforGeeks
entries.summary:
* Docker uses private registries (e.g. Harbor, AWS ECR) to store proprietary images.
* `docker pull` downloads an image from a registry, while `docker push` uploads a local image.
* Docker objects include images, containers, volumes, and networks.
* Images are read-only templates built from Dockerfiles, with layered architecture for efficient builds.
* Containers are runnable instances of images, isolated from each other and the host machine.
* Docker storage options include volumes, bind mounts, and tmpfs mounts for data persistence.
* Volumes are the preferred storage mechanism, managed by Docker and stored on the host filesystem.
* Docker networking provides isolation for containers, with types including bridge, host, overlay, none, and macvlan.
* Each container has its own filesystem, networking, and process space, and can be created, started, stopped, or deleted using the Docker API or CLI.

entries.title: TypeScript: JavaScript With Syntax For Types.
entries.summary:
* TypeScript is a strongly typed programming language that builds on JavaScript.
* TypeScript adds syntax for types to JavaScript, enabling better tooling and error checking.
* TypeScript code converts to JavaScript, which runs anywhere JavaScript runs.
* TypeScript uses type inference to provide great tooling without additional code.
* The `// @ts-check` comment enables TypeScript checking in JavaScript files.
* JSDoc comments can be used to provide type information in JavaScript files.
* TypeScript supports interfaces to describe the shape of objects and functions.
* The `interface` keyword is used to define a TypeScript interface.
* TypeScript provides auto-complete, editor checks, and other features to improve development.
* TypeScript can be adopted gradually, with types added incrementally to existing JavaScript projects.
* TypeScript is a superset of JavaScript that adds optional static typing.
* Interfaces are used to define the shape of objects, e.g., `interface Account { id: number; displayName: string; version: 1 }`.
* Type aliases can be used to define union types, e.g., `type Result = "pass" | "fail"`.
* TypeScript code is compiled to JavaScript, with types being removed in the process.
* Editor integration provides features like code completion, documentation, and issue detection.
* TypeScript supports various frameworks and libraries, including Angular, Vue, Jest, and Redux.
* TypeScript is widely adopted, with 78% of 2020 State of JS respondents using it.
* Ty

entries.title: TypeScript 教程 | 菜鸟教程
entries.summary:
* TypeScript 是 JavaScript 的超集，支持 ECMAScript 6 标准（ES6）。
* TypeScript 由微软开发，增加了静态类型检查的超集。
* TypeScript 设计目标是开发大型应用，可以编译成纯 JavaScript。
* TypeScript 的主要优势包括静态类型检查、强大的 IDE 支持、更好的代码可读性和现代 JavaScript 特性。
* TypeScript 不能直接在浏览器中运行，需要先编译为 JavaScript。
* TypeScript 编译器 (tsc) 在编译过程中进行类型检查，如果发现类型错误会报错并阻止编译。
* TypeScript 的核心增强能力包括类型批注、类型推断、类型擦除、接口、枚举、泛型和 mixin。
* TypeScript 支持 ES2015 的语法，包括类、模块、箭头函数、可选参数和默认参数。
* TypeScript 可以处理已有的 JavaScript 代码，并只对其中的 TypeScript 代码进行编译。
* TypeScript 与 JavaScript 代码可以一起工作无需任何修改
* TypeScript 通过类型注解提供编译时的静态类型检查
* TypeScript 可处理已有的 JavaScript 代码，并只对其中的 TypeScript 代码进行编译
* TypeScript 支持类型注解，如 `const hello: string = "Hello World!"`
* TypeScript 可以输出内容，如 `console.log(hello)`
* TypeScript 需要安装后才能使用
* TypeScript 是一种静态类型检查语言
* TypeScript 支持 JavaScript 代码的兼容性
* TypeScript 可以与其他编程语言一起使用，如 JavaScript、HTML、CSS
* TypeScript 提供了类型安全和编译时检查的功能

entries.title: TypeScript中文网 · TypeScript——JavaScript的超集
entries.summary:
* TypeScript is a superset of JavaScript that can be compiled to plain JavaScript.
* TypeScript is open-source and can run on any browser, machine, and operating system.
* TypeScript supports type checking, which helps catch errors and improves code quality.
* TypeScript is compatible with existing JavaScript code and libraries.
* TypeScript can be used with popular frameworks like Preact, Webpack, and Node.js.
* TypeScript has optional static typing, which can improve code maintainability and scalability.
* TypeScript supports meta-programming and has a powerful type system.
* TypeScript integrates well with tools like VS Code, providing features like IntelliSense and error messages.
* TypeScript can help reduce bugs and improve code clarity through type inference and rationalization.
* TypeScript is widely adopted and has a growing community of developers who use it for large-scale applications.
* TypeScript is a superset of JavaScript that adds optional static typing and other features.
* TypeScript supports the latest JavaScript features, including those from ECMAScript 2015 and future proposals.
* TypeScript code is compiled to plain JavaScript that can run on any JavaScript engine.
* TypeScript supports type inference, type checking, and code refactoring.
* TypeScript can be used with popular editors and IDEs, including Visual Studio, Visual Studio Code, Sublime Text, and Atom.
* The TypeScript compiler can be installed using npm with the command `npm install -g typescr

entries.title: FastAPI Tutorial: Build Your First Python REST API
entries.summary:
* The API returns a JSON error response `{"error": "Book not found"}` for missing resources.
* The `@app.post("/books/")` endpoint creates a new book using the `Book` Pydantic model.
* The `@app.get("/books/")` endpoint lists all books, with optional filtering by author using a query parameter.
* The `books_db` list stores book data in memory, but a proper database like SQLAlchemy or Tortoise-ORM is recommended for real projects.
* Error handling should be implemented using HTTP status codes.
* Authentication and authorization should be added for secure API access.
* The project should be structured into modules with routers to organize endpoints.
* FastAPI provides automatic validation and documentation features.
* Pydantic models are used to define the structure of API request and response data.
* The `return` statement is used to respond to API requests with JSON data.

* Visual Studio 2012 supports TypeScript

* TypeScript is an extension of JavaScript
* TypeScript code is compiled to JavaScript
* TypeScript supports object-oriented programming
* TypeScript is designed for large-scale JavaScript applications

entries.title: TypeScript Tutorial - GeeksforGeeks
entries.summary:
* TypeScript is a superset of JavaScript that adds static types for building scalable applications.
* Key TypeScript concepts include type annotations, interfaces, classes, union types, generics, and type inference.
* Setting up a development environment involves installing Node.js and TypeScript, and using an editor like Visual Studio Code.
* Fundamental syntax elements in TypeScript include type annotations, interfaces, and classes.
* Advanced topics in TypeScript include union types, generics, and type inference.
* Real-world projects, such as task managers and weather apps, can help reinforce knowledge of TypeScript.
* Staying updated with the official documentation and engaging with the community is essential for mastering TypeScript.
* A TypeScript Cheat Sheet can serve as a quick reference guide to essential concepts, features, and syntax.
* TypeScript is widely used with popular JavaScript-based frameworks and libraries, such as React and Angular.
* Mastering TypeScript involves building a portfolio of projects and staying informed about best practices and new developments.

entries.title: The Ultimate Guide to Prisma ORM + PostgreSQL (2025 Edition)
entries.summary:
* Prisma 6/7 uses a TypeScript-based engine for faster cold starts and smaller Docker images.
* TypedSQL allows writing raw SQL in .sql files with generated TypeScript types.
* Prisma Studio provides a visual dashboard for PostgreSQL data using `npx prisma studio`.
* Connection Pooling strings are required for hosted PostgreSQL providers like Supabase or Neon.
* `prisma.user.findMany()` fetches all users and includes their posts with `include: { posts: true }`.
* Raw SQL can be used with Prisma for more control over database queries.
* Prisma generates TypeScript types for SQL files.
* `JSON.stringify()` is used to log data in a readable format.
* Docker images are smaller with Prisma 6/7 due to the TypeScript-based engine.
* `npx prisma studio` is used to launch Prisma Studio for data visualization.
